title: Re-export libghostty types in phux-protocol (architectural correction)
description: Stop duplicating libghostty's enums (PhysicalKey/KeyAction/ModSet/MouseAction/MouseButton/Color/Underline/FocusEvent). Re-export them directly via 'pub use libghostty_vt::*' so phux automatically inherits upstream evolution. KeyEvent/MouseEvent struct wrappers stay because libghostty's are allocator-lifetime-bound. PasteTrust stays (phux concept, libghostty has only free functions in paste.rs). Drops the discriminant-divergence problem ADR-0006 amendment described. Single focused commit, no swarm.
acceptance_criteria: phux-protocol::input::key/mouse/focus uses libghostty types directly. phux-server::input::*_to_libghostty conversions collapse since enum types are identical. ADR-0008 written superseding 0002 (partial) + 0006 (partial). SPEC.md updated. cargo nextest run --workspace passes. clippy -D warnings clean. fmt clean.
close_reason: Done. ADR-0008 written. Re-exports landed: PhysicalKey/KeyAction/ModSet/MouseAction/MouseButton/FocusEvent/Color/RgbColor/PaletteIndex/Underline. Server-side conversions collapsed. 57/57 nextest pass, clippy/fmt clean. Net -887 lines (1396 deletions, 509 additions). Commit 194be01.

title: phux-protocol::input — FocusEvent + PasteEvent + PasteTrust
description: Implement the focus/paste types per SPEC §9.3/§9.4: FocusEvent { gained: bool }; PasteEvent { trust: PasteTrust, data: Vec<u8> }; PasteTrust = { Trusted, Untrusted }. Server gates DEC 1004 focus reports on terminal state; client always sends FocusEvent and server filters.
design:
New files in crates/phux-protocol/src/input/:
  focus.rs — FocusEvent { gained: bool }
  paste.rs — PasteEvent { trust: PasteTrust, data: Vec<u8> }, PasteTrust enum

Re-export from input/mod.rs.

PasteTrust mirrors libghostty's paste::PasteTrust. The trust bit is informational on the wire; the encoder decides bracketed-paste sequences (server-side responsibility per ADR-0006).
acceptance_criteria: All public types Debug+Clone+PartialEq+Eq. PasteTrust enum mirrors libghostty paste::PasteTrust. cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol passes. cargo clippy -p phux-protocol -- -D warnings clean.
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:879-898 — §9.3 INPUT_FOCUS
  SPEC.md:899-926 — §9.4 INPUT_PASTE
  ADR/0006-input-mirrors-libghostty.md

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol input::focus input::paste
  cargo clippy -p phux-protocol -- -D warnings
close_reason: Wave 1 of phux-6yl swarm. All four merged on main (commits fc60e7d, e7d27de, e29ec6f, 2ee9d92) + integration commit 7104b14. cargo fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean, 36/36 nextest pass.

title: phux-protocol::input — MouseEvent + ButtonState + MouseAction
description: Implement the mouse-path types per SPEC §9.2: MouseEvent { action: MouseAction, button: MouseButton, mods: ModSet, x: f64, y: f64 }. MouseAction = { Press, Release, Motion }. MouseButton = { None, Left, Middle, Right, ..., Button8 } matching libghostty mouse::Button. Coordinates are pane-local surface pixels (NOT cells) — required for SgrPixels-mode encoding (see SPEC §9.2.1 cell-geometry contract).
design:
New file: crates/phux-protocol/src/input/mouse.rs (sibling of key.rs from phux-6yl.1).
MouseEvent struct + MouseAction enum + MouseButton enum.

Re-export from crates/phux-protocol/src/input/mod.rs.

Numeric discriminants for MouseButton must match libghostty_vt::mouse::Button (similar to PhysicalKey↔Key in 6yl.1). Add a discriminant-match test.

Coordinates are f64 surface pixels in pane-local space. Document loudly that this is NOT cells — the server reconstructs cell positions using cell_w/cell_h from VIEWPORT_RESIZE (SPEC §10.5).
acceptance_criteria: All public types Debug+Clone+PartialEq+Eq. MouseButton numeric discriminants match libghostty_vt::mouse::Button (verified by a discriminant-match test). cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol passes. cargo clippy -p phux-protocol -- -D warnings clean.
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:819-860  — §9.2 INPUT_MOUSE
  SPEC.md:861-870  — §9.2.1 pixel positions / cell-geometry contract
  SPEC.md:1022-... — §10.5 VIEWPORT_RESIZE (provides cell_w/cell_h)
  ADR/0006-input-mirrors-libghostty.md

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol input::mouse
  cargo clippy -p phux-protocol -- -D warnings
close_reason: Wave 1 of phux-6yl swarm. All four merged on main (commits fc60e7d, e7d27de, e29ec6f, 2ee9d92) + integration commit 7104b14. cargo fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean, 36/36 nextest pass.

title: From impls for all four input event types + server-side KeyEncoder pipeline
description: In phux-server: implement field-for-field conversion from phux_protocol::input::{KeyEvent, MouseEvent, FocusEvent, PasteEvent} to the corresponding libghostty_vt::{key, mouse, focus, paste}::Event types. Wire the key encoder pipeline: per-pane KeyEncoder, set_options_from_terminal(&term) before each encode, encode_to_vec(&event, &mut buf). The encoder lives server-side; the wire never carries encoder options (ADR-0006). Mouse/Focus/Paste have their own libghostty encoders that follow the same pattern.
design:
New module: crates/phux-server/src/input/
  key.rs    — impl From<&phux_protocol::input::KeyEvent> for libghostty_vt::key::Event + PerPaneKeyEncoder
  mouse.rs  — impl From for MouseEvent + per-pane mouse encoder
  focus.rs  — impl From for FocusEvent + DEC 1004 gating
  paste.rs  — impl From for PasteEvent + bracketed-paste decision
  mod.rs    — re-exports

PerPaneKeyEncoder holds a libghostty_vt::key::KeyEncoder + reusable Vec<u8>. Calling sites: `encoder.set_options_from_terminal(&term); encoder.encode_to_vec(&event, &mut buf)`. Mouse/focus follow the same shape.

Encoder lives server-side. Never serialized to wire. See ADR-0006 rationale.

Conversion is infallible (libghostty event types are supersets / equal-sets of ours).
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: round-trip a KeyEvent through the From impl and assert all fields preserved. Conversion is infallible (no Result).
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:783-818 — §9.1.6 Server-side key encoding pipeline
  SPEC.md:871-878 — §9.2.2 Server-side mouse encoding pipeline
  ADR/0006-input-mirrors-libghostty.md (LOAD-BEARING — read fully before starting)

Depends on (compile-time): phux-6yl.1, phux-6yl.6, phux-6yl.7. All three must merge first.

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-server input::
  cargo clippy -p phux-server -- -D warnings
close_reason: Wave 2 of phux-6yl swarm. Linear history preserved (rebase + ff-only). Commits: phux-6yl.5 = 4eb7b94, phux-6yl.2 = ee81730. 69/69 nextest workspace pass, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean. ADR-0006 amended with KeyAction/Mods discriminant divergence + Rust orphan-rules note.

title: phux-protocol::input — KeyEvent + PhysicalKey + KeyAction + ModSet
description: Implement the key-path input types in phux-protocol per SPEC §9.1: KeyEvent { action: KeyAction, key: PhysicalKey, mods: ModSet, consumed_mods: ModSet, composing: bool, text: SmallVec<[u8;8]>, unshifted_codepoint: Option<char> }. PhysicalKey is the ~175-variant W3C-code-style enum; numeric discriminants must match libghostty_vt::key::Key. KeyAction = { Press, Release, Repeat }. ModSet is bitflags 2.x (SHIFT, CTRL, ALT, SUPER, CAPS_LOCK, NUM_LOCK + *_LEFT/_RIGHT side bits; no HYPER/META — libghostty collapses them to SUPER).
design:
Mirror the layout of crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/ (cell.rs, op.rs, grid.rs, compute.rs, mod.rs).

New module:
  crates/phux-protocol/src/input/
    key.rs     — PhysicalKey enum (~175 variants), KeyAction enum
    mods.rs    — ModSet bitflags (workspace bitflags = "2")
    event.rs   — KeyEvent struct (this task only adds KeyEvent; mouse/focus/paste land in sibling tasks)
    mod.rs     — re-exports

Re-export from crates/phux-protocol/src/lib.rs next to the existing `pub use diff::{...}` line.

PhysicalKey discriminants: read libghostty-rs/src/key.rs (resolved via Cargo.lock git rev). Each variant gets a literal `= N` discriminant matching libghostty's Key enum. The matching is verified by the test in the acceptance criteria.
acceptance_criteria: All public types Debug+Clone+PartialEq+Eq. Numeric PhysicalKey discriminants match libghostty_vt::key::Key. cargo nextest passes. cargo clippy -D warnings clean.
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:619-720  — §9.1 INPUT_KEY (full message + KeyEvent fields)
  SPEC.md:644-723  — §9.1.1 PhysicalKey enum (the authoritative list of variant names)
  SPEC.md:724-752  — §9.1.2 ModSet bits
  SPEC.md:753-762  — §9.1.3 consumed_mods semantics
  SPEC.md:763-767  — §9.1.4 composing flag
  SPEC.md:768-782  — §9.1.5 text + unshifted_codepoint
  ADR/0006-input-mirrors-libghostty.md — why we mirror 1:1

Verify:
  cd ~/workspace/phux && nix develop -c bash -lc '\
    cargo fmt --check && \
    cargo clippy -p phux-protocol -- -D warnings && \
    cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol'

Definition-of-done extra: a test physical_key_discriminants_match_libghostty() that enumerates every PhysicalKey variant and asserts `phux_protocol::PhysicalKey::Foo as u32 == libghostty_vt::key::Key::Foo as u32`. This is the cross-task validation gate.
close_reason: Wave 1 of phux-6yl swarm. All four merged on main (commits fc60e7d, e7d27de, e29ec6f, 2ee9d92) + integration commit 7104b14. cargo fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean, 36/36 nextest pass.

title: Server pane plumbing: PTY + per-pane diff loop
description:
Wire a real pane: spawn child PTY, feed bytes into libghostty_vt::Terminal, run grid::capture each frame, compute DiffOps, gate by FRAME_ACK/snapshot fallback per SPEC §6. Pools RenderState/RowIterator/CellIterator per pane (the spike allocates each call). Single-pane, no IPC yet — that's phux-byc.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `cargo run -p phux-server --example one_pane bash` spawns a real shell, accepts typed input from stdin, prints DiffOp summaries to stderr per frame.
- [ ] Steady-state capture loop allocates zero bytes (validated by criterion bench or dhat-rs heap trace).
- [ ] Clean shutdown on child exit (no zombie process, no panic on EOF).
- [ ] diff_spike example continues to print "replay invariant: snap0 + diff(0→1) == snap1   ✓" after the refactor.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p phux-server` green; clippy -D warnings clean.

title: Protocol foundation: input types + wire codec
description:
Translate SPEC §9 (input) and Appendix A (TLV framing) into Rust types and a length-prefixed codec. Closes the loop between phux_protocol and libghostty_vt by establishing the From<&Event> for libghostty_vt::*::Event boundary, with round-trip snapshot tests. Blocks all wire-format-dependent work (server runtime, client, transport).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A KeyEvent constructed in phux-protocol, converted via the server-side From impl, encoded by libghostty_vt's KeyEncoder, produces VT bytes that match committed insta hex snapshots for ≥10 representative cases (incl. ≥3 KIP enhancement levels).
- [ ] Arbitrary `Vec<DiffOp>` round-trips through the wire codec via proptest (≥1024 cases, no shrinks to failure).
- [ ] Insta hex-dump snapshots committed for ≥8 representative diff frames (small change visible in PR diff).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green; `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean.
- [ ] No libghostty types leak through phux-protocol's public API (verified by `cargo tree -p phux-protocol` not listing libghostty-vt).

title: phux-client: diff mirror — accumulate incoming Grid diffs into local state
description:
phux-client needs a local Grid mirror that consumes incoming diff frames from the server (CellRun/Clear/CursorMove/CursorStyle per phux-6yl.5) and produces a current-state Grid the renderer reads. Prerequisite for both attach-loop rendering AND predictive echo (9gw.1).

## Required Skills
rust,vt-rendering,libghostty
design:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:407-440 §8 hot-path frame model
  SPEC.md:487-535 §8.3 Diff operations
  SPEC.md:536-567 §8.4 Snapshots
  ADR/0002-diff-based-protocol.md

Module: crates/phux-client/src/mirror/
  state.rs    — DiffMirror { grid: libghostty Grid, cursor: Cursor, frame_id: u64 }
  apply.rs    — fn apply(state: &mut DiffMirror, ops: &[DiffOp])
                  per-op: CellRun → write cells at (row, col),
                          Clear → blank N cells from (row, col),
                          CursorMove → update cursor,
                          CursorStyle → update cursor attrs.
  snapshot.rs — fn ingest_snapshot(state: &mut DiffMirror, snap: &Snapshot)
                  full-state reset (used on first ATTACH or recovery).

Replay invariant test (mirrors diff_spike from the server side):
  Given a starting Grid G0, a server-produced diff D such that G0.apply(D) = G1,
  the client's DiffMirror starting at G0 applies D and produces a Grid
  byte-identical to G1.

  Reuse the server's diff_spike example output as a fixture if practical.

Tests in crates/phux-client/src/mirror/tests.rs (or tests/mirror_replay.rs):
  - apply CellRun + read back exact bytes
  - apply Clear over a populated row, assert blanked
  - CursorMove updates cursor; CursorStyle propagates attrs
  - Random-walk proptest: arbitrary sequence of DiffOps applied to an
    empty Grid produces the same Grid as applying them via the server's
    capture+diff path (round-trip with the server-side replay invariant)
acceptance_criteria: DiffMirror::apply is invariant w.r.t. server-side replay (client mirror after applying diff bytes == server Grid that produced them). Proptest passes. cargo nextest run -p phux-client green.

title: Integration test: server + synthetic client message exchange
description:
End-to-end harness that spins up a phux-server process (or in-process runtime), drives it with a synthetic client over the wire codec, and asserts the full HELLO → ATTACH → INPUT → diff-stream → DETACH lifecycle. Captures the byc epic's overall acceptance criteria.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,integration-testing
design:
New: crates/phux-server/tests/end_to_end.rs

Harness approach (in-process, no subprocess fork):
  let socket = tempdir().path().join('phux.sock');
  let server = tokio::spawn(phux_server::runtime::run_with_shutdown(socket.clone(), shutdown_rx));
  // give it a moment to bind
  let client = UnixStream::connect(&socket).await?;
  let (mut reader, mut writer) = phux_protocol::codec::framed(client);

Scenarios to assert (one #[tokio::test] each, or one parameterized):

1. handshake_roundtrip:
   - Client sends HELLO with proto version
   - Server responds HELLO_ACK with negotiated capabilities (SPEC §6)
2. attach_returns_snapshot:
   - Client sends ATTACH(session='default')
   - Receives ATTACHED with SessionId + a pane snapshot frame
3. input_routes_to_pane:
   - After ATTACH, send INPUT_KEY('a')
   - Assert the pane's input log contains the encoded byte (stub from byc.4)
4. detach_clean_shutdown:
   - Send DETACH; receive DETACHED; the connection drains; no panics.
5. server_survives_mid_frame_disconnect:
   - Client writes a partial frame (half a length prefix) then drops the
     stream. Server must NOT panic; the per-client task exits cleanly.

Then shutdown_tx.send(()).await; server.await??; assert socket file unlinked.

Note: this test depends on byc.3 (runtime+socket) and byc.4 (ATTACH/DETACH).
Should be the LAST byc task to land.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 scenarios pass. cargo nextest run -p phux-server --test end_to_end green. No flakes under --test-threads=1.

title: phux-server: ATTACH/DETACH lifecycle + multi-client keystroke merge
description:
Implement the ATTACH/DETACH message handlers and the multi-client merge semantics: multiple clients attached to the same session see the same pane, with their keystrokes interleaved in arrival order at the PTY.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,ipc
design:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:238-282 §7.1/7.2 Client/Server message catalog (ATTACH, ATTACHED)
  SPEC.md:283-308 §7.3 DETACH/DETACHED
  SPEC.md:955-1015 §10.1-10.2 Sessions/Panes

Builds on byc.3's per-client task and byc.1's Registry.

Server-side state:
  pub struct AttachedClient {
      id: ClientId,
      session: SessionId,
      tx: mpsc::Sender<ServerFrame>,  // server → client outbound frames
  }
  pub struct ServerState {
      registry: phux_core::Registry,
      attached: HashMap<ClientId, AttachedClient>,
      pane_subscribers: HashMap<PaneId, Vec<ClientId>>,
  }

ATTACH handler:
  - On ATTACH(session_name), look up SessionId (or create if not found and
    allowed by attach flags), register the AttachedClient in pane_subscribers
    for the active pane.
  - Send ATTACHED response with SessionId + initial pane snapshot.

DETACH handler:
  - Remove ClientId from pane_subscribers; close mpsc tx; respond DETACHED.

INPUT_KEY / INPUT_MOUSE / INPUT_FOCUS / INPUT_PASTE handlers:
  - For each input message from an attached client, route it to the
    server-side per-pane channel that ultimately writes to the PTY (PTY
    wiring lives in phux-bc1.2; for now route to a stub Pane::ingest_key
    method that just records the event).
  - Order: a SINGLE mpsc per pane consumes inputs from all attached
    clients. Whatever ordering the tokio scheduler delivers IS the
    arrival order — this is the multi-client merge contract.

Test (crates/phux-server/tests/multi_client_merge.rs):
  - Spin up server with one session/window/pane
  - Connect two synthetic UnixStream clients, both ATTACH same session
  - Each client sends a sequence of distinct INPUT_KEY events
  - Assert: the per-pane input log contains every key from both clients
    exactly once, in some interleaving (not strictly deterministic, but
    every key appears).
acceptance_criteria: ATTACH/DETACH round-trips. Two attached clients on the same session both see the same active pane. Per-pane input log records every keystroke from both clients exactly once. Integration test green.

title: phux-server: tokio current-thread runtime + Unix socket listener
description:
Wire phux-server's main loop on a tokio current-thread runtime. Bind a Unix domain socket at $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/phux/phux.sock (or fallback), accept client connections, hand each off to a per-client task that speaks the wire codec from phux-6yl.4.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,unix-sockets
design:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:96-116 §4 Transport (UDS, framing assumptions)
  SPEC.md:117-149 §5 Framing (length-prefixed TLV from phux-6yl.4/5)

New: crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs
  pub struct ServerRuntime { registry: phux_core::Registry, socket_path: PathBuf, ... }
  pub fn run(socket_path: PathBuf) -> Result<(), ServerError>:
    - Build tokio Builder::new_current_thread().enable_all().build()
    - block_on(async move { serve_loop(...).await })

serve_loop:
  - Bind UnixListener::bind(socket_path)
  - Loop: accept().await → spawn client task
  - On SIGINT/SIGTERM (use tokio::signal): drain accept loop, drop listener,
    unlink socket file. Existing client tasks get a cancellation token.

Per-client task:
  - Frame reader: phux_protocol::codec::FrameReader<UnixStream read half>
  - Frame writer: phux_protocol::codec::FrameWriter<UnixStream write half>
  - For byc.3 specifically: just LOG every incoming frame and respond with
    PONG to PING. Full message routing comes in byc.4.

Socket path resolution: \$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/phux/phux.sock if set,
else /tmp/phux-\$UID/phux.sock. Create the parent dir with 0700 perms.

Refuse to run if the socket file already exists AND a process holds it
(connect-probe). If stale, unlink and proceed.

Tests:
  - integration test in crates/phux-server/tests/socket_lifecycle.rs:
    spawn server in a temp dir, connect a tokio UnixStream, send a PING,
    read PONG, shut server down via the cancellation token, assert socket
    file is removed.
acceptance_criteria: Server binds UDS, accepts a connection, round-trips PING→PONG. Clean shutdown unlinks socket. Integration test in tests/socket_lifecycle.rs green.

title: phux-core: Layout tree (split-h/v, kill-pane, focus-direction)
description:
Replace byc.1's placeholder linear LayoutNode with a real binary-tree layout (per SPEC §10.3) supporting split-horizontal, split-vertical, kill-pane, and directional focus traversal.

## Required Skills
rust,layout-tree,domain-modeling
acceptance_criteria: Layout supports split-h/v, kill, focus-direction. pane_rects tiles the window. Proptests for invariants. cargo nextest run -p phux-core green.

title: phux-core: SessionId/WindowId/PaneId + SlotMap registries
description:
Define the stable opaque ID newtypes (SessionId, WindowId, PaneId) and the SlotMap-backed registries that own Session/Window/Pane records. Pure domain types — no IO, no async.

## Required Skills
rust,slotmap,domain-modeling
acceptance_criteria: phux-core compiles. Registry CRUD covered by tests. SessionId/WindowId/PaneId are distinct types (rustc rejects mixing). cargo nextest run -p phux-core green.

title: Selection / copy-mode protocol + libghostty selection APIs
description:
tmux copy-mode is one of its defining features; we need a peer. libghostty PR #12794 (merged 2026-05-24) adds selection APIs to libghostty-vt's C surface: select_word, select_line, select_all, select_output, format_buf, selection_adjust, selection_order, selection_contains, selection_equal. Wire these through phux's protocol so clients can drive copy-mode against the canonical server-side selection state.

Defer until libghostty-rs wraps the new C APIs (track via libghostty-rs commits — they were already integrating at the time of phux's wave 1).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Wire format additions in SPEC.md: SELECTION_BEGIN, SELECTION_ADJUST, SELECTION_FORMAT_REQUEST, SELECTION_FORMAT_RESPONSE, SELECTION_CLEAR. Per-pane, per-client (each client has its own selection).
- [ ] phux-server bridges to libghostty_vt::selection::* (once libghostty-rs wraps them).
- [ ] phux-client renders selection as a visual overlay on the diff mirror.
- [ ] Copy-mode keybind (default `Ctrl-b [`) enters selection mode; arrows adjust; Enter copies to clipboard via libghostty_vt clipboard OSC.
- [ ] Rectangular selection (Mosh's `RECT` order from PR #12794) supported.
- [ ] Semantic-prompt-aware select-output (jump from prompt to prompt) works.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green; clippy -D warnings clean.

## Out of scope for this epic

- Search-in-buffer (different feature, file separately later).
- Vim-style motion commands inside copy-mode (post-v0.1 UX polish).
- Yank-buffer history (tmux's `paste-buffer` list).

## Watch / unblock signal

libghostty-rs adds a 'selection' module wrapping the PR #12794 C surface. Until then this epic stays deferred.

title: Predictive local echo (Mosh-class UX), client-side
description: Implement Mosh-style predictive local echo in phux-client: client maintains a prediction overlay against its diff mirror, dim-renders predicted cells, reconciles on server FRAME_ACK (SPEC §6). Transport-agnostic — Unix socket today, QUIC later. Mandated by ADR-0007.
design:
Module: crates/phux-client/src/predict/
  state.rs    — PredictionState { pending: VecDeque<Prediction>, mirror: Grid }
  overlay.rs  — render predictions over the diff mirror (dim attribute)
  reconcile.rs — on FRAME_ACK, walk pending predictions and drop confirmed ones; on mismatch, snap mirror to authoritative state

Algorithm (Mosh-style, on our cell diffs):
  1. User key event K arrives client-side.
  2. Send K upstream; locally apply guess(K) to a copy of the mirror.
  3. Render guess cells with PREDICTION attribute (dim).
  4. On FRAME_ACK from server (matched by frame_id), compare server cells
     in the affected range with our prediction.
       match → drop prediction, render normally
       mismatch → drop prediction, render authoritative
  5. On timeout (no ACK within N ms) → drop prediction.

PredictionPolicy enum: Always, Adaptive (RTT-based), Never. Configurable via TOML (phux-nz4).

Reference: Mosh SSP paper, section on prediction.
acceptance_criteria: Typing over a 200ms-RTT simulated link feels instant. Predictions render dim, reconcile within one FRAME_ACK. Snapshot test for the prediction overlay state machine. cargo nextest run -p phux-client passes; clippy -D warnings clean.
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:150-161 — §6 FRAME_ACK (the reconciliation signal)
  ADR/0007-mosh-class-transport-and-satellites.md — load-bearing rationale

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-client predict::
  cargo clippy -p phux-client -- -D warnings
  Manual: `tc qdisc add dev lo root netem delay 200ms` then type rapidly

title: Spawn PTY child + feed bytes into Terminal
description: Use portable-pty (or rustix-openpty) to spawn a real shell, read its output in a loop, feed bytes into libghostty_vt::Terminal via vt_write. Wire to the per-pane capture pool from T2.1; emit DiffOp[] each frame to a debug sink (stdout) for now. No IPC yet (E3).
acceptance_criteria: phux-server example 'one_pane' runs a real bash, types commands, and prints DiffOps to stderr. Clean shutdown on child exit.
notes:
Depends on phux-bc1.1 (PaneCapture API).

Manual verify:
  cargo run -p phux-server --example one_pane bash
  # Type 'ls', 'echo hi', resize via SIGWINCH propagation later (out of scope here)
  # Exit shell cleanly; example should print summary and return 0

Automated verify (smoke):
  cargo build -p phux-server --examples
  cargo clippy -p phux-server --examples -- -D warnings

title: Pool RenderState/RowIterator/CellIterator per pane
description: grid::capture currently allocates fresh RenderState/RowIterator/CellIterator each call (fine for the spike). For the per-pane hot loop these must be held across frames. Add a PaneCapture struct that owns them; grid::capture becomes a thin wrapper for one-shot uses (tests, phux capture CLI).
acceptance_criteria: Per-pane benchmark shows zero allocations in steady-state capture loop (criterion or cargo-bench).
notes:
Spec refs (informational, no API changes):
  SPEC.md:407-440 — §8 hot-path / frame model
  SPEC.md:413-440 — §8.1 frame model

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-server
  cargo run -p phux-server --example diff_spike   # must still print replay invariant ✓
  cargo bench -p phux-server --bench capture      # steady-state regression check

title: Diff wire codec — CellRun/Clear/CursorMove/CursorStyle + insta hex snapshots
description: Implement encode/decode for the four DiffOps and their nested Cell/Color/Underline/CellFlags atoms. Add insta hex-dump snapshot tests for a representative set of frames so changes to the wire format are visible in PR diffs.
design:
Builds on phux-6yl.4's wire scaffold.

New file: crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/diff.rs
  Encode/decode for: DiffOp (CellRun, Clear, CursorMove, CursorStyle), Cell, Color (Default|Indexed|Rgb), Underline, CellFlags.

Color encoding: 1-byte tag (0=Default, 1=Indexed, 2=Rgb) + payload.
CellFlags: 16-bit little-bitset over the bitflags 2 representation.
Cell.text: u8 grapheme count + 4-byte little-endian char codepoints.

New test file: crates/phux-protocol/tests/diff_wire_snapshots.rs
  Insta hex-dump snapshots (use the same hex_dump helper pattern as phux-6yl.3).

  Cases (≥8):
    - empty diff
    - single CellRun (1 cell, ASCII)
    - CellRun (5 cells with mixed fg/bg, BOLD+ITALIC)
    - CellRun with non-Default underline
    - Clear (single row, full width)
    - CursorMove
    - CursorStyle (visible=true, Block, blink=false)
    - mixed sequence: CellRun + CursorMove + Clear

Proptest in src/wire/diff.rs (#[cfg(test)]):
  proptest! { fn roundtrip(ops in any::<Vec<DiffOp>>()) { ... } }
acceptance_criteria: Proptest roundtrip for arbitrary DiffOp Vec. ≥8 insta hex snapshots covering CellRun (1/n cells), Clear, CursorMove, CursorStyle.
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:441-486  — §8.2 Cells (the type layout being encoded)
  SPEC.md:487-535  — §8.3 Diff operations
  SPEC.md:1300-1340 — Appendix A primitives

Depends on phux-6yl.4 (consumes the Frame/FieldId/Encoder/Decoder primitives).

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol --test diff_wire_snapshots
  cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol wire::diff::tests
  cargo insta review
close_reason: Wave 2 of phux-6yl swarm. Linear history preserved (rebase + ff-only). Commits: phux-6yl.5 = 4eb7b94, phux-6yl.2 = ee81730. 69/69 nextest workspace pass, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean. ADR-0006 amended with KeyAction/Mods discriminant divergence + Rust orphan-rules note.

title: Wire codec scaffold: TLV framing per SPEC Appendix A
description: Big-endian, length-prefixed frames with custom TLV. Field IDs per Appendix A. Encoder writes into &mut Vec<u8> (or bytes::BytesMut); decoder takes &[u8] and returns Result<(Frame, &[u8] remaining), DecodeError>. Module: crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/.
design:
New module: crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/
  frame.rs       — Frame header (length-prefixed, big-endian per SPEC Appendix A)
  field.rs       — TLV field ID constants matching SPEC §7
  encode.rs      — Encoder writes into &mut bytes::BytesMut
  decode.rs      — Decoder takes &[u8], returns Result<(FrameKind, &[u8] tail), DecodeError>
  error.rs       — thiserror DecodeError enum
  mod.rs         — public surface

Add `bytes = "1"` to workspace dependencies. Use BytesMut for the encoder buffer.

Decoder must never panic on malformed input — every length read is bounds-checked. The proptest in acceptance criteria validates this.

This task scaffolds the codec. Per-frame variants (HELLO, INPUT_KEY, DIFF_FRAME, etc.) land in follow-ups — but the FrameKind enum should be declared (with at least HELLO + PING as proofs of concept) so the boundary is exercised end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: Proptest: encode→decode roundtrip for every Frame variant. Malformed input never panics (fuzz-style assertions).
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:117-149  — §4 Transport (length prefix, big-endian)
  SPEC.md:150-161  — §5 Framing (TLV structure)
  SPEC.md:223-282  — §7 Message catalog (field IDs)
  SPEC.md:1300-1340 — Appendix A Encoding primitives

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol wire::
  cargo clippy -p phux-protocol -- -D warnings
close_reason: Wave 1 of phux-6yl swarm. All four merged on main (commits fc60e7d, e7d27de, e29ec6f, 2ee9d92) + integration commit 7104b14. cargo fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean, 36/36 nextest pass.

title: Key-encode snapshot test: assert produced VT bytes match insta snapshots
description: Build representative KeyEvents (plain 'a', Ctrl+C, Shift+Tab, Alt+Enter, KIP-progressive arrow with all flags) and assert the bytes produced by the server-side encoder match insta hex-dump snapshots. Covers the KIP/fixterms/modifyOtherKeys matrix at the boundary.
design:
New test file: crates/phux-server/tests/key_encode_snapshot.rs

Hex-dump helper (private):
  fn hex_dump(bytes: &[u8]) -> String  // 16 cols per row, offset|hex|ascii

For each case, build a KeyEvent, run it through the encoder from phux-6yl.2, format with hex_dump, assert_snapshot.

Cases (≥10):
  - plain ASCII 'a' press
  - Ctrl+C
  - Shift+Tab
  - Alt+Enter
  - Esc
  - F1, F12 with modifyOtherKeys=2
  - Up arrow under KIP-disabled
  - Up arrow under KIP report_all_keys=on
  - composing=true dead key
  - Release event under KIP report_event_types=on

Snapshots committed under crates/phux-server/tests/snapshots/.
acceptance_criteria: ≥10 snapshot cases including at least 3 distinct KIP-enhancement levels. snapshots committed under crates/phux-server/tests/snapshots/.
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:783-818 — §9.1.6 encoding pipeline (defines the KIP enhancement levels)

Depends on phux-6yl.2 having landed (encoder exposed via phux_server::input::key::PerPaneKeyEncoder).

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-server --test key_encode_snapshot
  # Review pending snapshots:
  cargo insta review

title: Client + TUI rendering composition
description:
phux-client: connect to server, accumulate diffs into local Grid mirror, render via crossterm or direct VT writes. Sends INPUT_KEY/INPUT_MOUSE/INPUT_FOCUS/INPUT_PASTE upstream. The attach loop.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `phux attach <session>` shows a working pane that re-renders cleanly under SIGWINCH (terminal resize).
- [ ] Keypresses (incl. KIP-enhanced sequences from the host terminal), mouse events, focus events, and bracketed paste all propagate to the server and round-trip back through the inner program.
- [ ] Reconnect after server restart auto-reuses the existing session (no orphan PTY).
- [ ] First-frame TTFP under 50ms on local socket transport.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p phux-client` green; clippy -D warnings clean.

title: Multiplexer core: Session/Window/Pane domain + IPC
description:
phux-core domain types with SlotMap-based stable IDs (SessionId, WindowId, PaneId), a layout tree, attach/detach lifecycle. phux-server runtime exposes them over the wire format from phux-6yl via Unix domain socket. tokio current-thread runtime throughout.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Two `phux attach <session>` invocations against the same server see the same pane and have their keystrokes merged in arrival order.
- [ ] Detach (Ctrl-b d equivalent) preserves session; reattach within 30s shows current state via initial snapshot then diff stream.
- [ ] Layout ops (split-horizontal, split-vertical, kill-pane, focus-direction) work via IPC and update all attached clients.
- [ ] Server survives client disconnect mid-frame (no crash, no leaked FDs).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p phux-core -p phux-server` green; integration test spins up a server, attaches a synthetic client, asserts message exchange.

title: Status bar composition + client render integration
description:
Compose widgets from [[status.widgets]] into a horizontal status strip, render it as the final row of the client TUI. Hooks into phux-client's render path.

## Required Skills
rust,tui,vt-rendering
design:
crates/phux-config/src/statusbar.rs:
  pub struct StatusBar {
      widgets: Vec<Box<dyn Widget>>,
      separator: String,  // default ' | '
      align: Vec<Alignment>, // per-widget: Left/Center/Right
  }
  impl StatusBar {
      pub fn build(cfg: &StatusCfg, registry: &WidgetRegistry) -> Result<Self, WidgetError>;
      pub fn render(&self, ctx: &WidgetContext, width: u16) -> Vec<Cell>;
        // Pad/truncate to width, group by alignment.
  }

Integration in phux-client:
  - On startup, load Config (phux-nz4.2) → build StatusBar
  - Reserve the bottom row of the terminal for the bar
  - Re-render the bar on a tick (default 1s or per-widget poll_interval)
    OR whenever an event the widgets care about fires

Snapshot test (crates/phux-client/tests/statusbar_render.rs):
  - 80-col render with time + session-name + spacer + something right-aligned
  - Truncation behavior at 20-col width
  - Empty widget list → no status bar drawn (just an empty row)

Note: depends on the client having a render loop (phux-9gw epic in flight).
For this task, it's OK to ship a self-contained example
(crates/phux-client/examples/statusbar_demo.rs) that renders to stdout
without the full attach loop, and reserve the full integration for the
phux-9gw epic to wire up. Update the task if 9gw lands first.
acceptance_criteria: StatusBar::build + render produce expected output snapshots. Truncation behavior correct. Demo example runs. cargo nextest run -p phux-config -p phux-client green.

title: Status bar Widget trait + 2 in-tree widgets (time, session-name)
description:
Define the Widget trait and WidgetSpec deserialization contract. Ship 'time' and 'session-name' as in-tree widget implementations that demonstrate the plugin API.

## Required Skills
rust,plugin-architecture
acceptance_criteria: Widget trait shipped. WidgetRegistry instantiates widgets from WidgetSpec. 'time' and 'session-name' both render to expected WidgetCells (snapshotted). cargo nextest run -p phux-config green.

title: Keybind resolver: match KeyEvent against [[keybinds]] entries
description:
Parse keybind strings like 'C-b c' or 'M-S-Tab' into a normalized form that can be matched against phux-protocol::KeyEvent values. Snapshot-test the match table.

## Required Skills
rust,parser-combinators
acceptance_criteria: 'C-b c'-style strings parse to KeyChord sequences. Resolver matches multi-chord prefixes correctly. cargo nextest run -p phux-config green. Insta snapshots committed.

title: Config loader: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME defaults + JSON-spec round-trip
description:
Resolve config at $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/phux/phux.toml (or ~/.config/phux/phux.toml), fall back to embedded defaults on missing-file, and validate the 'agents can read/write config' premise via a JSON↔TOML round-trip test.

## Required Skills
rust,toml,xdg
acceptance_criteria: load() resolves XDG path with sensible default fallback. JSON→Config→TOML→Config round-trip is identity. cargo nextest run -p phux-config green.

title: New crate phux-config + TOML schema + line:col error reporting
description:
Bootstrap a new crate phux-config with the Config struct (per DESIGN.md), serde-toml parsing, and human-readable error reporting that points to file line:col on malformed input.

## Required Skills
rust,toml,serde
acceptance_criteria: phux-config crate added to workspace. Config parses example from DESIGN.md. Malformed input produces ConfigError pointing to line:col. cargo nextest run -p phux-config green.

title: Set GitHub repo topics + about metadata
description:
Add discoverability topics + repo description so the project surfaces in github search.

## Required Skills
gh-cli
design:
One-shot gh CLI invocation:

  gh repo edit phall1/phux \
    --description 'A terminal multiplexer built on libghostty-vt' \
    --homepage 'https://phall1.github.io/phux' \
    --add-topic rust \
    --add-topic tmux \
    --add-topic libghostty \
    --add-topic terminal-multiplexer \
    --add-topic kip \
    --add-topic kitty-keyboard-protocol

Trivial; can be batched into the same commit as a38.3's workflow PR.
Done as a chore-type bd issue rather than a task because no code lands.
acceptance_criteria: github.com/phall1/phux page shows the 6 topics, description, and homepage URL.

title: Publish phux-protocol v0.0.1 placeholder to crates.io for docs.rs
description:
Get docs.rs builds working for phux-protocol so external users can read the wire-format docs. Requires removing publish=false from phux-protocol, bumping version, and either gating or vendoring the libghostty-vt git dep (crates.io disallows git deps in published crates).

## Required Skills
rust,cargo-publish,docs.rs
design:
Sub-steps:

1. Decide libghostty-vt handling:
   - Option A: Make libghostty-vt an OPTIONAL dep behind a 'server' feature.
     phux-protocol public API uses re-exported types only when 'server' is
     enabled. Default features OMIT libghostty-vt. docs.rs builds default
     features → no git dep → publishable.
   - Option B: Wait for libghostty-vt to land on crates.io upstream.
   - Option C: Carve a no-libghostty subset of phux-protocol (likely too
     invasive given ADR-0008's 'use libghostty types directly').
   Recommend A.

2. crates/phux-protocol/Cargo.toml:
   - Remove publish = false
   - Bump version = '0.0.1' (override workspace.package.version locally,
     or bump workspace.package.version and let phux-protocol inherit while
     other private crates keep publish = false).
   - Add [features] section: server = ['libghostty-vt']
   - Move libghostty-vt from dependencies to optional dep with no default
     feature, or under [target.'cfg(...)'.dependencies].

3. Cargo.toml workspace: add docs.rs metadata to phux-protocol:
   [package.metadata.docs.rs]
   all-features = true

4. Local verify:
   cargo publish -p phux-protocol --dry-run
   cargo doc --no-deps -p phux-protocol
   (with and without --features server)

5. cargo publish -p phux-protocol (live, requires CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN).

6. Confirm https://docs.rs/phux-protocol/0.0.1 renders.

Caveat: this is the first place we'd hit the consequence of phux-protocol
re-exporting libghostty types in its PUBLIC api (ADR-0008). The 'server'
feature gating is the standard escape hatch. Document this in ADR-0008.
acceptance_criteria: docs.rs/phux-protocol/0.0.1 renders the type docs. 'cargo publish -p phux-protocol --dry-run' clean. Other workspace crates stay publish=false.

title: mdBook deploy: SPEC + ADRs to GitHub Pages
description:
Set up mdBook with SPEC.md + ADR/*.md as content, deploy to GitHub Pages via a new workflow that triggers on push to main.

## Required Skills
mdbook,github-actions
design:
New: book.toml at repo root (or docs/book.toml) configuring mdBook with:
  - src = SPEC.md + ADR/*.md (use a SUMMARY.md that lists them)
  - title, authors from workspace metadata
  - theme: default; output html only

New: .github/workflows/docs.yml
  - Trigger: push to main
  - Single job, ubuntu-latest
  - Steps: checkout, install mdbook (cargo install or peaceiris/actions-mdbook),
    build, peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3 deploy to gh-pages branch.
  - permissions: contents: write (for the gh-pages push).

Repo settings step (manual or via gh CLI in a chore commit):
  gh api repos/phall1/phux/pages -X POST -f source[branch]=gh-pages -f source[path]=/

SUMMARY.md (drives mdBook navigation):
  # Summary
  - [Spec](SPEC.md)
  - [Architecture Decisions](ADR/README.md)
    - [0001 Language: Rust](ADR/0001-language-rust.md)
    - ... (etc, one per ADR)
acceptance_criteria: mdBook builds locally via 'mdbook build' from repo root. docs.yml workflow green on push to main. https://phall1.github.io/phux renders SPEC + all 8 ADRs.

title: ADR: phux vs Mux positioning (protocol substrate, not orchestration app)
description: From research/2026-05-25-awesome-libghostty-scan.md. Short ADR explaining that phux is a terminal-multiplexer protocol substrate, not an agent-orchestration product. coder/mux solves the orchestration UX and terminates terminals at an in-process WASM renderer; phux is the wire-protocol layer that products like Mux could ship on top of. Goal: prevent scope confusion when the swarm story matures and contributors propose merging the two niches.

title: Spec RolePolicy on ATTACH/COMMAND (primary/viewer + takeover)
description: From research/2026-05-25-awesome-libghostty-scan.md. Spec a primary/viewer exclusivity model with takeover semantics on SPEC §11 ATTACH/COMMAND. Modeled on psyclyx/vanish's role primitive. Targets the agent-watching-agent UX where one client owns input and others observe; takeover should be a deliberate command rather than implicit. Wire-format change requires a SPEC edit in the same PR.

title: Research artifact: awesome-libghostty competitive scan
description: Capture the awesome-libghostty landscape scan as a research doc in research/. Documents closest neighbors (vanish, zmx, hauntty, ykmx, footty, coder/mux, coder/ghostty-web), what to steal (vanish primary/viewer role model, Mux product vocabulary), and confirms no spec invalidation. Lives in research/ — not an ADR, since no decision is being made. Surfaces two follow-ups: (1) RolePolicy in ATTACH/COMMAND, (2) ADR on phux-vs-Mux positioning.
close_reason: Research artifact landed at research/2026-05-25-awesome-libghostty-scan.md on branch research/awesome-libghostty-scan. Two follow-ups filed.

title: Satellite / federation v0.2 — hub-and-spoke across hosts
description:
Multi-host phux: one phux-server acts as hub, exposes sessions from peer phux-servers (satellites) over QUIC and SSH-stdio transports. Killer use case: AI-agent sandboxes — spin up disposable VMs, attach to all of them from one local hub.

Deferred to v0.2. ADR-0007 captures the design and the v0.1 must-not-preclude invariants so this stays additive.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `phux satellite add devbox ssh://devbox` registers a satellite via SSH-stdio.
- [ ] `phux ls` from the hub shows local and satellite sessions, namespaced as `local://name` and `sat://devbox/name`.
- [ ] Attaching to a satellite session works transparently — same input, diff, FRAME_ACK semantics as a local pane.
- [ ] Satellite disconnect mid-frame: hub buffers input, pane goes amber, reattach within 30s replays via snapshot fallback.
- [ ] QUIC transport (quinn) implemented and exercised in CI integration tests.
- [ ] Hub does NOT re-encode VT bytes; encoders stay per-satellite per ADR-0006.
- [ ] No regressions to single-host v0.1 ergonomics (Unix socket transport remains default).

## Out of scope for this epic

- Mesh topology (rejected in ADR-0007).
- Mosh wire-compatibility (rejected in ADR-0007).
- Direct client → satellite shortcuts (NAT-punching, discovery, etc.).
- Central registry / OIDC auth.

title: GitHub Actions CI: fmt + clippy -D warnings + nextest + cargo-deny
description: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs on push/PR. Uses dtolnay/rust-toolchain with rust-toolchain.toml. Caches target/ and ~/.cargo. Gates merge on fmt --check, clippy -D warnings, cargo nextest run, cargo deny check.
design:
New file: .github/workflows/ci.yml

Triggers: push to main, pull_request.

Jobs (all use nix develop wrapper so CI matches local exactly):
  - fmt:    nix develop -c cargo fmt --check
  - clippy: nix develop -c cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
  - test:   nix develop -c cargo nextest run --workspace
  - deny:   nix develop -c cargo deny check
  - doc:    nix develop -c env RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --no-deps --workspace

Setup actions (in order on each job):
  - actions/checkout@v4
  - DeterminateSystems/nix-installer-action@main
  - DeterminateSystems/magic-nix-cache-action@main
  - Swatinem/rust-cache@v2  (key includes Cargo.lock + rust-toolchain.toml)

Matrix: ubuntu-latest only initially. macOS added once a contributor needs it.

No --no-verify shortcuts. No skipping flaky tests.
acceptance_criteria: Green CI on a no-op PR. Total runtime <5min on cold cache.
notes:
Depends on phux-a38.1 (need a github remote).

Verify:
  - Create a no-op PR (touch CHANGELOG.md), push, watch CI pass green
  - Total wall time <5min on cold cache
  - gh pr checks <PR#> shows fmt/clippy/test/deny/doc all green

title: Publish phux to github.com/phall1/phux + push
description: Create the public GitHub repo (gh repo create phall1/phux --public --source=. --remote=origin --push). README/LICENSE/CONTRIBUTING are already in place. Add a one-line repo description and 'rust, tmux, libghostty, terminal-multiplexer, kip' topics.
design:
One command:
  gh repo create phall1/phux \
    --public \
    --source=. \
    --remote=origin \
    --push \
    --description "A libghostty-based terminal multiplexer — generation-ahead of tmux."

Then add topics:
  gh repo edit phall1/phux \
    --add-topic rust \
    --add-topic tmux \
    --add-topic libghostty \
    --add-topic terminal-multiplexer \
    --add-topic kip \
    --add-topic kitty-keyboard-protocol

Confirm gh is auth'd (gh auth status). Repo must not already exist (gh will error if it does — that's fine, treat as success and just push).
acceptance_criteria: https://github.com/phall1/phux reachable; main branch matches local; topics set.
notes:
Verify:
  gh repo view phall1/phux --json url,visibility,description,repositoryTopics
  git remote -v | grep origin
  git ls-remote origin main
close_reason: Pushed to https://github.com/phall1/phux on 2026-05-25 (commit 36e07ac). Repo public; 8 commits on top of LICENSE root. Topics: not yet set — folded into phux-a38.2 work.

title: Release readiness: CI + GitHub + docs site
description:
GitHub Actions: fmt/clippy -D warnings/nextest/cargo-deny/doc gated. Publish to phall1/phux on GitHub. README/CONTRIBUTING polish. mdBook for SPEC + ADRs.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] github.com/phall1/phux is public with green CI on main.
- [ ] Every PR runs fmt, clippy -D warnings, nextest, cargo-deny, cargo doc — and merging is blocked on all five.
- [ ] CI wall time under 5min on cold cache.
- [ ] docs.rs builds successfully for phux-protocol once a 0.0.1 placeholder is published.
- [ ] mdBook deploys SPEC + ADRs to GitHub Pages on every push to main.
- [ ] Repo has topics: rust, tmux, libghostty, terminal-multiplexer, kip, kitty-keyboard-protocol.

title: Config (TOML) + widget-first status bar
description:
TOML config loader with schema (per DESIGN.md), keybind resolver, status-bar architected as a widget/plugin system from day one (default config is just one composition of widgets). Validates the 'agents read/write the config' premise.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `~/.config/phux/phux.toml` loads at startup. Missing file → sensible defaults. Malformed file → clear error pointing to line:col.
- [ ] An agent can programmatically generate a valid phux.toml from a JSON spec (round-trip test in tests/).
- [ ] Status bar is composed from a `[[status.widgets]]` list. ≥2 widgets ship in-tree (`time`, `session-name`) as examples of the WidgetSpec contract.
- [ ] Keybinds resolve correctly under modifier combinations matching libghostty key-encode (snapshot test).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p phux-config` green; clippy -D warnings clean.
